Common.tagline: Your senses remember less than you think.

Home.heroTitle: Your senses remember less than you think.
Home.heroSubtitle: Six tiny games. Each shows you one thing — a duration, a colour, a pitch, a quantity, an angle, a position — then takes it away and asks you to reproduce it. Score 0–50. It is humbling.
Home.sectionGamesSub: Pick a dimension. Five rounds each.
Home.how1Body: A stimulus appears for a moment — watch, listen, or feel the timing.
Home.how2Body: It disappears. Now reproduce it from memory using a single, focused control.
Home.how3Body: Each round is scored 0–10 on a perceptual curve. Five rounds make 50.
Home.statsNote: No account needed. Your scores are saved on this device and, optionally, to the global leaderboard.

Consent.title: Cookies & ads
Consent.message: We use a cookie to remember your settings, and (outside premium) show ads on content pages to keep Senso free. Choose what you're comfortable with.

Pricing.subtitle: Senso is free forever. Premium removes ads and unlocks training analytics and private tournaments.
Pricing.notConfigured: Payments are not configured in this deployment yet.

About.title: About Senso
body: Senso is a small collection of perceptual precision games. Each one isolates a single dimension of perception — time, colour, pitch, quantity, or orientation — shows you a stimulus, removes it, and asks you to reproduce it from memory.

The idea is simple and a little humbling: our senses feel vivid and exact in the moment, but memory of a raw sensation decays fast. Senso turns that fragility into a game you can measure, share, and slowly improve at.

Every game is free and works without an account. Scores are computed on a shared perceptual curve so a 40/50 means roughly the same effort whether you're matching a colour or a duration. Daily challenges are identical for everyone in the world, and challenge links replay the exact same targets.
Senso is built as an open, extensible engine: adding a new sense is implementing one interface. If you have an idea for a seventh game, we'd love to hear it.

Privacy.title: Privacy
body: Senso is designed to need as little of your data as possible. You can play every game without an account.

Local storage: we store a random device identifier, your chosen display name, your best scores, and your cookie/ad preference in your browser's local storage. This never leaves your device unless you submit a score.

Leaderboards: when you submit a score, we send the game, mode, seed, your guesses, your display name and device identifier to our server, which recomputes and stores the result. We may store a coarse country derived from your connection. We never store your IP address with the score.
Accounts (optional): if you sign in, we store your email and a premium flag. Payments are handled entirely by Stripe; we never see your card details.
Ads & cookies: on content pages we may show ads via Google AdSense, but only after you accept in the consent banner. Premium users see no ads. You can change your choice at any time.
Contact us to request deletion of any data associated with your account or device identifier.

Terms.title: Terms of use
body: Senso is provided as-is, for entertainment and curiosity. By using it you agree to these terms.
Fair play: leaderboard scores are recomputed on the server from your seed and guesses. Attempting to forge scores, automate play, or abuse the service may result in removal of your scores.
Premium: subscriptions renew automatically until cancelled. You can cancel any time from the billing portal; access continues until the end of the paid period. Statutory rights are unaffected.
Content: the long-form articles on Senso are for general education and are not professional medical or psychological advice.
We may update these terms; material changes will be noted on this page.

Scoring.title: How scoring works
Scoring.intro: Every game uses the same scoring curve so difficulty feels comparable across senses. Each round is worth 0–10 points; five rounds make a maximum of 50.
Scoring.formulaBody: We measure your error in each game's natural perceptual unit, then convert it to points with score = 10 × 0.5^(error ÷ half). At zero error you get 10. At the game's "half-score error" you get exactly 5. At twice that, 2.5 — and so on, never dropping below 0.
Scoring.unitsBody: Errors are measured where perception actually lives: log2 of the duration or count ratio (Weber's law), ERB-rate for pitch, CIEDE2000 ΔE for colour, and shortest circular distance for angle. This is why a small mistake on a long duration costs the same as the same proportional mistake on a short one.
Scoring.bandsBody: Calibrated so a first-timer lands around 29/50 and an expert reaches the high 30s to low 40s. A clean 45+ is genuinely rare.
Scoring.fairnessBody: Targets are generated deterministically from a seed. The daily challenge uses the same seed for everyone on a given day, and challenge links replay an exact set of targets — so the leaderboard compares like with like.

NotFound.title: Lost the thread
NotFound.body: That page doesn't exist — your sense of direction may be as fallible as the rest.
